executive_summary: PRD-001 covers five user stories (US-001 through US-005) targeting the async job queue infrastructure: Pydantic models, SQLite-backed queue, submit_job() helper, Python worker process, and ProgressReporter. Implementation is largely complete — all five modules exist with correct logic. Two notable gaps were found: (1) FR-2 requires the DB path to be overridable via the PARALLAX_DB_PATH environment variable, but queue.py only exposes a db_path keyword argument with no env-var resolution; (2) the aiosqlite dependency is gated behind the [server] extra and is not installed in the default uv environment, causing all 38 queue-related tests to fail with ModuleNotFoundError in a stock uv run pytest invocation. A minor semantic inconsistency also exists: the enqueue() method sets status='pending' while US-004-AC01 refers to status=='queued', though the worker handles both.

verification_by_fr.notes: JobData, JobResult, and PythonProgress all subclass pydantic.BaseModel with strict type annotations. Tests confirm instantiation and field types.

Default DB path ~/.config/parallax/jobs.db is correctly implemented. However, the PARALLAX_DB_PATH environment variable override specified in the PRD is absent from queue.py — only the db_path keyword argument is supported.

submit_job() uses asyncio.run() only for the quick DB enqueue (sub-ms), then immediately returns the job ID. The worker is spawned with start_new_session=True in a fully detached Popen, so the caller is never blocked on inference.
worker.py builds the pipeline command as [data.uv_path, 'run', 'python', <script_path>] — the uv_path field from JobData is correctly used to resolve the uv binary.
Both ProgressReporter.update() and .done() call print(..., flush=True), satisfying the flush-on-every-write requirement.

None of the new modules (server/jobs.py, server/queue.py, server/submit.py, server/worker.py, comfy_diffusion/progress.py) import torch or comfy.* at module top level. aiosqlite is imported inside get_queue() at call time.

verification_by_us.notes: server/jobs.py exports JobData, JobResult, PythonProgress as Pydantic BaseModel subclasses with all required fields and correct types. 25 tests pass.

Code implementation is correct (singleton, table schema, all five public methods). However, all 38 US-002 tests fail in CI with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'aiosqlite' because the dependency is gated behind the [server] extra and not auto-installed by uv sync. Also, PARALLAX_DB_PATH env-var override (FR-2) is missing.

server/submit.py exports submit_job(data: JobData) -> str. Enqueues via get_queue(), spawns worker.py with start_new_session=True, passes job_id as sole CLI argument. Mock-based performance tests pass.

worker.py reads JobData, spawns pipeline subprocess using uv_path, streams NDJSON progress via update_progress(), sets status='completed' with output_path on exit 0, and 'failed' with stderr on non-zero exit. Minor: enqueue sets status='pending'; AC01 says 'queued' — worker handles both via _PROCESSABLE_STATUSES.

comfy_diffusion/progress.py exports ProgressReporter with update() and done(). All AC tests pass: single-line JSON, flush=True, pct=100.0 in done(), optional kwargs forwarded correctly. Existing imports unaffected.

minor_observations: enqueue() stores status='pending' while US-004-AC01 references status=='queued'. The worker guards against this by accepting both via _PROCESSABLE_STATUSES. Recommend standardising to 'queued' or updating the PRD language to 'pending'.

submit_job() spawns worker.py using sys.executable (the venv Python) rather than uv. This is functional and correct since worker.py itself uses data.uv_path for the pipeline; however it means the worker is not sandboxed to the project venv automatically on all systems.
queue.py has an update_progress() method beyond the AC04 spec — this is additive and intentional (used by worker). No compliance concern.
The _reset_singleton() helper exposed in queue.py is test-only. Consider marking it with a leading underscore (already done) and a clear docstring (already done). No action needed.

aiosqlite is declared under [project.optional-dependencies.server] which is correct design, but test discovery will silently fail without installing that extra. Consider adding a pytest conftest.py skip/xfail marker or updating CI to use uv run --extra server pytest.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is functionally sound for four of five user stories and five of six functional requirements. Two actionable fixes are needed before this iteration can be approved: (1) Add PARALLAX_DB_PATH environment variable support to queue.py to satisfy FR-2 — a one-line os.environ.get('PARALLAX_DB_PATH') lookup in the _DB_PATH initialisation is sufficient; (2) Ensure the test suite installs the [server] extra so queue tests can run — either via uv run --extra server pytest in CI configuration or by adding aiosqlite to dev dependencies. A third cosmetic fix (status value 'pending' vs 'queued' terminology) is recommended for semantic clarity but is not blocking.
